description_long: This is a twin-engine propeller airplane with a high-wing configuration and a robust, utilitarian design. The fuselage is predominantly white with a blue nose and red accents on the tail and engine nacelles, suggesting a possible use for transport, patrol, or utility purposes. The aircraft features a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer mounted low on the tail. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the crew. The landing gear appears to be a tricycle configuration, with the main gear under the wings and a nose gear under the cockpit. The overall structure is streamlined for aerodynamic efficiency, with straight wings and no visible winglets.
description: A white, blue-nosed, twin-propeller high-wing airplane with red tail accents and a forward cockpit, designed for utility or transport roles.
description_view_0: Side view showing the high-mounted straight wings, twin propeller engines, and the red-accented tail.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the full wingspan, twin engines, and the single vertical stabilizer of the tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same high-wing, twin-engine layout and the blue nose.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the blue nose, forward cockpit windows, and the symmetrical placement of the propeller engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and low-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units under the wings and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
